# Opt-in variable-overrides comment

**Date:** 2026-06-24
**Status:** Approved

## Problem

Every file rendered from a template carries two autogenerated comment lines:

```yaml
# This file is autogenerated from https://github.com/Invoca/web/blob/-/templates/.../foo-deploy.jsonnet
# Variable overrides used: {"deploySha":"c1b207207834863ba96ffd130f1b1d03c0406672"}
```

`deploySha` is different on every build, so the `# Variable overrides used:` line
changes on **every render of every file** — including files (e.g. a Service) that
never reference `deploySha`. When a render touches many files, this produces a
large, content-free diff that buries the real changes.

Example diff (from a Buildkite render):

```diff
-# Variable overrides used: {"deploySha":"c1b207207834863ba96ffd130f1b1d03c0406672"}
+# Variable overrides used: {"deploySha":"9d3377a17f4b2806aa0c3ed337ebcb9a70811416"}
```

## Out of scope

The same example diff also shows the `# This file is autogenerated from ...` URL
churning, because the linter pipeline renders out of an ephemeral Buildkite clone
(`/buildkite/builds/kite-<uuid>/.../temp_clone_repo/deployment/templates/...`) and
that absolute prefix is embedded verbatim. **This is being fixed on the caller
side** (the linter pipeline will render from a stable, repo-relative path), not in
this gem. The `# This file is autogenerated from ...` line is untouched by this
change.

## Goal

Make the `# Variable overrides used:` comment **opt-in**. Suppress it by default so
the diff noise disappears everywhere immediately, and add a CLI flag to re-enable it
when the override metadata is useful (e.g. debugging).

## Design

### Behavior

- **Default:** the `# Variable overrides used: {...}` comment is **not** emitted.
- **`--include-variable-overrides`:** emit the comment, exactly as it is emitted
  today (only when overrides actually exist).
- **`--no-include-variable-overrides`:** explicit form of the default (suppress).

The `# This file is autogenerated from ...` comment is always emitted, unchanged.

### Threading

A single boolean follows the exact path that `variable_overrides` and `source_repo`
already take through the call chain:

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `cli_arguments.rb` | Add `:include_variable_overrides` to the `CLIArguments` Struct; add an `include_variable_overrides?` predicate returning `!!include_variable_overrides`. |
| `cli.rb` | Add `op.on("--[no-]include-variable-overrides", ...) { args.include_variable_overrides = _1 }`; pass `include_variable_overrides: args.include_variable_overrides?` into `TemplateDirectoryRenderer.new`. |
| `template_directory_renderer.rb` | Accept `include_variable_overrides:` kwarg (default `false`), store it, pass it to `ResourceSet.new`. |
| `resource_set.rb` | Accept `include_variable_overrides:` kwarg (default `false`), store it, pass it to `Resource.new` on the `standard_resources` path (line ~191). |
| `resource.rb` | Accept `include_variable_overrides:` kwarg (default `false`), store it, pass it to `template_klass.render(...)`. |
| `template.rb` | `render` class method and `initialize` accept `include_variable_overrides:` (default `false`), store it; `variable_override_comment` returns `""` unless the flag is on. |

### Gating point

The only logic change is in `Template#variable_override_comment` (template.rb:43-49).
It currently returns the comment whenever `variable_overrides&.any?`. It will return
`""` unless **both** the flag is on **and** overrides exist:

```ruby
def variable_override_comment
  if include_variable_overrides && variable_overrides&.any?
    <<~EOS
    # Variable overrides used: #{variable_overrides.to_json}
    EOS
  end || ""
end
```

Everything else is plumbing.

## Scope notes

- The deploy-grouped path (`deploy_grouped_resource.rb:31`) never passes
  `variable_overrides` to its `Resource.new`, so those files already emit no
  override comment and need no flag. Left unchanged.
- This is a default-behavior change to a released gem (currently `0.2.5`). It
  includes a **version bump to `0.3.0`** and a `CHANGELOG.md` entry under both
  `### Added` (the flag) and `### Changed` (the comment is now opt-in).

## Testing

- Update `spec/kubernetes_template_rendering/template_spec.rb:56` (currently asserts
  the override comment is present) to pass `include_variable_overrides: true`.
- Add coverage for both states:
  - flag off (default) → `# Variable overrides used:` is **absent** even when
    overrides exist.
  - flag on → comment is **present** when overrides exist.
- Confirm the `# This file is autogenerated from ...` line is still present in all
  cases.
